content.claim: Rising SEN identification in Hungary is partly driven by definitional and categorization changes rather than solely by changes in underlying prevalence, as Hungary moved from an 'organic versus non-organic' dichotomy that did not accurately link diagnoses to actual service needs
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Causal attribution of definitional change to quantitative trend effect is inferred rather than directly measured
Timing of definitional shifts relative to identification growth not specified
No comparison with pre- and post-definition identification rates provided
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
